task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window is open displaying the contents of the file 'alice_in_wonderland.txt'.
The Sugar Write application window is visible on the screen, identifiable by its specific toolbar and text editing area.

task_completion.description: Set the document title to 'Alice Chapter 1'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document title field, typically located at the top of the Sugar Write window, displays exactly 'Alice Chapter 1'.

task_completion.description: Begin typing the required passage into the document
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text area in Sugar Write contains at least the first half of the required passage (e.g., 'Alice was beginning to get very tired of sitting by her sister on the bank...').

task_completion.description: Complete the required passage in the document
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text area in Sugar Write contains the entire required paragraph exactly as specified, ending with '...without pictures or conversations?'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GUI to enter text
integrity.visual_evidence: The text is visibly entered into the Sugar Write graphical interface, rather than being written to a file via terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent used Sugar Write, not a generic text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The application containing the final text is definitively Sugar Write, not a standard text editor like gedit, nano, or vim.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to bypass manual entry
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing Python, bash, or other scripts being executed to automatically generate or inject the document.
